Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are going to model the product design and launch process using DECLARE constraints Based on the scenario we can derive the following declarative rules

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We will assume that TFC and CE can be in any order But for DECLARE we might not model every possibility

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the process flow IG to DD to TFC and CE in any order to AG to PC to LT and UT in any order to MP to FL

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: However note the scenario says approval as a step after testing Maybe we are missing an activity Given the ambiguity I will stick to the activity descriptions

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So the DECLARE model will be built on the following assumptions The process starts with IG Then DD Then TFC and CE can occur in any order Then AG must occur after DD TFC and CE Then PC must occur after AG Then LT and UT can occur in any order Then MP must occur after the testing Then FL

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We can model the following DECLARE constraints Existence every activity must occur Init IG must be the first activity Response after IG there must be DD after DD there must be TFC and CE after AG there must be PC after PC there must be LT and UT after MP there must be FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: To satisfy both precedence constraints AG must occur after both TFC and CE So we can model precedence TFC AG and CE AG Similarly for the testing phase LT MP and UT MP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This matches the scenario TFC and CE might be done in parallel so any order is acceptable Similarly LT and UT can be in any order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore the DECLARE model declare model equals existence IG DD TFC CE AG PC LT UT MP FL init IG response IG DD DD TFC DD CE AG PC PC LT PC UT MP FL precedence TFC AG CE AG LT MP UT MP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We set support 1 0 and confidence 1 0 for all
